name: NUMT_chr5_major_mtDNA_insert
description: Largest known human nuclear mitochondrial DNA insertion (NUMT) on chr5q33.1, ~5.8 kb of mtDNA-derived sequence at ~97-99% identity to chrM. Reads from chrM can map spuriously here (and vice versa). Hazkani-Covo et al. 2010 catalogued thousands of NUMTs genome-wide; this is among the largest single contiguous ones.

architecture.internal_structure: ~5.8 kb fragment of mitochondrial genome (covers a large arc of chrM); flanking sequence is single-copy nuclear; one of >700 NUMTs identified in human genome.

related_diseases: Spurious 'heteroplasmy' calls from NUMT contamination in mtDNA sequencing
